summary: PR #2496 adjusted `lb_8001B14C` in `src/melee/lb/lbcardnew.c` by removing a local `lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries` alias and writing directly through `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries`. The PR body says this improved the target code shape and left the function at 98.77124%, with the remaining diff described as an `r5`/`r6` allocation swap in a compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop. The PR was closed with no merged_at value in the loaded metadata.

key_files.role: Only changed source file; modified `lb_8001B14C` snapshot entry writes by replacing a local pointer alias with direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` accesses.

smart_moves.text: Tested a source-shape change that preserved typed field access but altered aliasing: direct global aggregate member writes replaced a cached pointer alias, improving the reported decomp score for the target function.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` accesses match target shape more closely
text_corpus bot report: `lb_8001B14C` improved from 63.23% to 98.77%

smart_moves.text: Kept the remaining blocker explicit rather than claiming full match: the PR body records a remaining `r5`/`r6` allocation swap in the compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Remaining blocker section

decomp_lessons.lesson: In `lb_8001B14C`, a local pointer alias to `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` produced a worse code shape than direct global member indexing; for similar unmatched code, test whether preserving direct aggregate access better matches the original aliasing/codegen.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removes `snapshot_entries` alias and writes directly to `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries[...]`
human_pr_text: states this matched target code shape much more closely
text_corpus bot report: score changed from 63.23% to 98.77%

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a loop copies linked-list node fields into a global/table-like destination, aliasing choices can affect register allocation even if the C semantics are equivalent.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: remaining diff is an `r5`/`r6` allocation swap in the compiler-unrolled linked-list copy loop
raw_diff: loop copies `time`, `file_no`, and `blocks` from `(*copy_scan)` to snapshot entries

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Remove a local pointer alias and repeat the full global-member access path when matching codegen suggests the original source did not cache the member pointer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: direct member access replaces `snapshot_entries[...]`
human_pr_text: direct accesses match target code shape much more closely
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Evidence is specific to this PR and function; do not generalize into a global rule that direct globals are always preferable to local aliases.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Document partial-match blockers at the register-allocation level when a function is close but not matched.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: `lb_8001B14C`: 98.77124%, remaining `r5`/`r6` allocation swap
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Loaded evidence does not include objdiff output beyond the PR body and bot score report.

source_update_candidates.title: Alias-shape choices can be matching-relevant
source_update_candidates.text: Consider noting under matching tactics or authored-source-style guidance that replacing repeated aggregate access with a local pointer alias, or vice versa, can materially affect MWCC register allocation/code shape; prefer the shape supported by local objdiff evidence rather than assuming aliases are always cleaner.
evidence_refs: PR #2496 body states direct `lb_80432A68.snapshot_entries` accesses matched the target code shape much more closely
PR #2496 bot report shows `lb_8001B14C` improved from 63.23% to 98.77%
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR provides a concrete example where a source-level alias choice affected matching. Curator should decide whether this is already covered by existing standards or merits a small addition.

rejection_notes: Do not promote a broad rule that direct global access is preferable to local pointer aliases; PR evidence is limited to one function and one global member.
Do not claim `lb_8001B14C` matched fully; the PR body explicitly lists it as 98.77124% with a remaining register-allocation blocker.
Do not infer reviewer approval or merge acceptance; no review comments were loaded and metadata has `merged_at`: null.
